title: baccharis integrifolia
intro: Baccharis integrifolia, also known as the mountain silver bush or silver ragwort, is a shrub native to the western United States and Mexico, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Baccharis integrifolia include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Baccharis integrifolia include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally, depending on the specific condition being treated.
